response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web software developer with strong general engineering experience, but the role demands deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears anywhere in the resume. His performance work (low-latency DLang services at Sociomantic) is server-side systems tuning, not silicon or accelerator architecture, and there is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD experience, or architectural modeling. He meets the formal education-plus-experience bar, but the domain mismatch is fundamental rather than bridgeable.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the Bachelor's + 8 years qualification path
Genuine low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec) showing performance-oriented thinking
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, robotics work, audio/wave-field synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, training/inference optimization, or GPU architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level silicon performance architecture

key_factors.factor: AI accelerator and DL workload expertise
